target.name: Corintis
target.founder: Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, Elison Matioli

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Corintis is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Remco van Erp, Sam Harrison, and Elison Matioli, having raised $24M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 same-stage private peers in Chips & Semiconductors (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) plus 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), though the public comps cannot be directly applied without revenue disclosure.

Two triangulation methods yield actionable results. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implying $48M from the $24M capital base. Peer-median anchoring is more material: the 4 private comparables span $500M to $5.0B, with a median of $1.5B. Public semiconductor stocks trade at a 24.4x price-to-sales median, but absent Corintis revenue data, this method is not deployable. The public peer set suggests outsized valuation multiples in the sector, though direct application remains impossible.

Triangulation across the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $41M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $774M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around whether Corintis aligns with earlier-stage comparable profiles or the more mature, well-capitalized peers like Etched ($5.0B). Key uncertainties include product differentiation, capital efficiency relative to peers, and the stage-appropriate valuation anchor. The median of $774M is defensible as a mid-point between organic stage progression and peer positioning.
